rationale: I split Agriculture & Food Science into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (crops, animals, soils, food, water, tech) with actionable granularity for readers. I separated applied breeding/genetics from general agronomy because breeders and field agronomists read different literatures, and I split food science from food safety/microbiology for similar reasons. I avoided creating ultra-narrow niches (e.g., individual commodity specialities like 'soybean physiology') and did not include plant molecular biology or food policy (those belong to Biology and Public Health, respectively). Some boundaries are judgment calls — e.g., placing irrigation under its own card versus inside soil/agronomy — but I favored distinct operational disciplines that working professionals would use when introducing themselves.

subspecialties.name: Agronomy & Crop Science
subspecialties.tagline: Field crop management, yield optimization, cropping systems
covers: Applied crop production, rotations, nutrient management, weed management
Examples: cereal crop systems, fertilizer trials, tillage and row-spacing studies
explicitly_excludes: crop-breeding-genetics — breeding/genetics and molecular trait development
soil-science-fertility — in-depth pedology and soil chemistry studies

subspecialties.name: Crop Breeding & Genetics
subspecialties.tagline: Plant breeding, genetic improvement, marker-assisted selection
covers: Quantitative & molecular breeding, GWAS for agronomic traits, cultivar development
Examples: QTL mapping for drought tolerance, genomic selection trials
explicitly_excludes: plant-pathology-protection — pathogen biology and control studies
plant-biology-mechanism (Biology) — basic cellular/molecular plant physiology

subspecialties.name: Plant Pathology & Protection
subspecialties.tagline: Crop diseases, diagnostics, integrated disease management
covers: Epidemiology, diagnostics, fungicide/biocontrol trials, quarantine pathogens
Examples: rust disease surveys, pathogen resistance screening, disease modeling
explicitly_excludes: entomology-pest-management — insect pests and biological control
plant-biology-mechanism (Biology) — basic host–pathogen molecular mechanism

subspecialties.name: Soil Science & Fertility
subspecialties.tagline: Soil health, chemistry, nutrient cycling, fertility management
covers: Pedology, nutrient dynamics, carbon sequestration, amendment evaluations
Examples: soil organic matter studies, fertilizer fate, erosion control research
explicitly_excludes: irrigation-water-management — hydrology and irrigation system design
agronomy-crop-science — field crop management trials (operational focus)

subspecialties.name: Entomology & Pest Management
subspecialties.tagline: Insect pests, integrated pest management, biological control
covers: Pest ecology, monitoring, insecticide trials, IPM strategies, pollinator studies
Examples: pest thresholds, trap designs, biological control agent releases
explicitly_excludes: plant-pathology-protection — non-insect pathogens and disease control
agronomy-crop-science — operational crop management where pest control is one component

subspecialties.name: Horticulture & Fruit/Vegetable Production
subspecialties.tagline: High-value crops, orchards, greenhouse and horticultural systems
covers: Cultivation and management of fruits, vegetables, ornamentals, greenhouse systems
Examples: greenhouse nutrient solutions, pruning trials, postharvest handling of produce
explicitly_excludes: postharvest-supply-chains — downstream handling, logistics and storage
plant-breeding-genetics — breeding of horticultural crops (goes to breeders)

subspecialties.name: Animal Science
covers: Ruminant and monogastric production systems, feed studies, reproduction, welfare
Examples: feed efficiency trials, housing systems, herd management research
explicitly_excludes: veterinary-medicine (not included) — clinical animal disease treatment (goes to Veterinary or Medicine)
aquaculture-fisheries — aquatic animal production

subspecialties.name: Aquaculture & Fisheries
subspecialties.tagline: Aquatic farming, fish health, breeding, and fisheries management
covers: Aquaculture production systems, feed, disease control, stock assessment
Examples: recirculating aquaculture studies, hatchery breeding, wild fishery assessments
explicitly_excludes: animal-science — terrestrial livestock production
marine-biology (Biology) — basic marine ecosystem research

subspecialties.name: Agricultural Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Farm machinery, structures, mechanization and postharvest tech
covers: Machine design, storage structures, drying technologies, mechanization, power systems
Examples: combine harvester design, grain drying improvements, on-farm processing equipment
explicitly_excludes: precision-agriculture-remote-sensing — sensing/analytics and decision-support tech
postharvest-supply-chains — logistics and cold-chain management

subspecialties.name: Irrigation & Water Management
subspecialties.tagline: Water supply, irrigation systems, watershed and drainage management
covers: Irrigation design, scheduling, water use efficiency, drainage, salinity management
Examples: drip irrigation trials, watershed modeling for agricultural runoff
explicitly_excludes: soil-science-fertility — broader pedology and soil chemistry studies
agricultural-engineering — hardware design work (if primarily engineering-focused)

subspecialties.name: Precision Agriculture & Remote Sensing
subspecialties.tagline: Sensing, GIS, robotics, data-driven farm decision support
covers: Drones, satellite remote sensing, sensor networks, farm data analytics, robotics
Examples: variable-rate application algorithms, yield mapping, machine-learning for crop stress
explicitly_excludes: agricultural-engineering — mechanical design of farm machinery (hardware-centric)
computer-science (CS) — algorithm-only papers without agricultural application

subspecialties.name: Food Science & Technology
subspecialties.tagline: Food processing, formulation, product development, quality
covers: Food chemistry, processing technologies, shelf-life extension, novel foods
Examples: emulsification methods, plant-protein texturization, sensory analysis of new products
explicitly-excludes: food-safety-microbiology — microbiological hazard studies and pathogen control
nutrition-science — human dietary intervention and metabolism studies

subspecialties.name: Food Safety & Microbiology
subspecialties.tagline: Foodborne pathogens, contamination control, spoilage microbiology
covers: Microbial hazards, HACCP, detection methods, thermal processing validation
Examples: Listeria control in plants, pathogen detection assays, shelf-life microbiology
explicitly-excludes: food-science-technology — non-microbial processing or formulation work

subspecialties.name: Nutrition Science
subspecialties.tagline: Human and animal nutrition, dietary studies, feed formulation
covers: Nutrient requirements, feed evaluation, human dietary intervention related to food composition
Examples: micronutrient bioavailability trials, livestock feed digestibility studies
explicitly-excludes: public-health-nutrition (Public Health) — population-level dietary policy studies

uncovered_concerns: Commodity-specific cards (e.g., 'Wheat Science', 'Soybean Research') were considered but rejected to avoid proliferation and to keep the grid MECE and broadly usable.
Veterinary clinical medicine is closely adjacent to Animal Science but was excluded because clinical treatment and pathology belong under Veterinary/Medicine rather than Agriculture & Food Science.
Plant molecular biology and mechanistic cellular plant studies were deliberately excluded and redirected to Biology to keep this level applied and practitioner-focused.
Food policy and population nutrition policy were excluded and routed to Public Health despite overlap with food systems research.

Some engineers and computer scientists prefer 'Agri-Informatics' or 'Food Engineering' labels; I used 'Precision Agriculture & Remote Sensing' and 'Agricultural Engineering' to match common practitioner self-identifiers.
